issues: Draft says 'Boston University won 7-2' but the box score shows BU scored goals 2,3,4,6,7 and ND scored goals 1,5,8 — making the final score BU 5, ND 3, not 7-2.

Draft omits the key Sockeyes connection: Chris Dickson (former Richmond Sockeyes player) is named as the North Dakota goalie in the box score, and Dave Tomlinson (former Richmond Sockeyes player) is named as a BU skater — these are the primary reasons this clipping is in the Sockeyes archive.
Draft mentions 'Tony Amonte scored twice' — while Amonte did score twice, the Sockeyes-relevant hook (Dickson, Tomlinson) is entirely missing from the description.
No headline was found in the OCR, which is consistent with the draft leaving it blank — this is acceptable.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Box score from a March 1990 college hockey game in which ex-Sockeyes Dave Tomlinson (Boston University) and goalie Chris Dickson (North Dakota) both appeared; BU won 5-3.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Box score and brief game note from a Boston University vs. North Dakota college hockey game played around March 18, 1990, published in the Grand Forks Herald. The final score was BU 5, North Dakota 3, with Tony Amonte scoring twice for BU and Dixon Ward contributing two assists for ND. Former Richmond Sockeyes Dave Tomlinson skated for Boston University and drew a roughing penalty in the first period, while former Sockeyes netminder Chris Dickson started in goal for North Dakota, stopping 33 of 38 shots. A brief note describes Amonte breaking down the right wing on a 2-on-1 rush with linemate Tomlinson.
review.notes: The Sockeyes relevance is strong: both Dave Tomlinson and Chris Dickson are confirmed roster members of the 1987 Sockeyes. The AI draft entirely missed this connection and also miscalculated the final score (5-3, not 7-2 — goals 1 and 5 are ND, goals 2/3/4/6/7 in the draft numbering include goal 8 for ND, yielding BU 5, ND 3).
